function.name: mcp_agentcall_configure_inbound_ai
function.description:
Set up an AI voice agent to autonomously answer incoming calls to a phone number. This is the AgentCall equivalent of Twilio's inbound voice webhook URL: instead of pointing the number at a webhook you host, you give AgentCall a system prompt and AgentCall handles the call with a managed AI voice agent. Use this (NOT 'create_webhook') when you want a phone number to answer calls. Set the system prompt, voice, and optional first message; when someone calls the number, the AI picks up and follows the prompt. Pass `notify.emailTo` to also receive a plain-English summary of every call (caller, intent, urgency, callback time) so small-business owners do not need to read transcripts. Free tier: 5 minutes/month free trial, no card required. Once the trial is exhausted, inbound AI calls hang up until the 1st of next month (UTC) or until the user upgrades to Pro for unlimited inbound AI at $0.40/min. Available on US and Canada numbers. Before writing systemPrompt from scratch, fetch GET https://api.agentcall.co/v1/calls/prompt-templates (no auth) for pre-built templates: receptionist, lead qualifier, appointment booker, customer support, call screener. Preview voices via GET https://api.agentcall.co/v1/calls/voices.

IMPORTANT (persistent config: confirm before enabling, monitor after): this is NOT a one-shot tool call. Once configured, the number autonomously answers every incoming call from then on, accumulates per-minute charges (at $0.40/min on Pro past the Free 5-minute monthly trial), and keeps doing so until disable_inbound_ai is called. Before invoking, get the user's explicit confirmation on: (1) exact E.164 number to configure (read it back), (2) systemPrompt content (read back at least the WHAT WE DO and PRICING sections so the user can correct invented facts before any caller hears them), (3) record setting (default false; recording adds $0.01/min and 1-year retention), (4) notify.emailTo (confirm the address is theirs and that they want every call summarized), (5) maxDurationSecs (use the smallest value that fits the use case so a stuck call cannot run for an hour), (6) budget expectations (Free 5 min/month then hang-up, or Pro $0.40/min with no monthly cap). After enabling, periodically check get_usage so the user sees consumption, and proactively offer to call disable_inbound_ai when their stated goal is met (testing finished, campaign over, business closed for the season). On reports of unexpected charges or unexpected calls, disable_inbound_ai first then debug.

function.name: patch
function.description:
Targeted find-and-replace edits in files. Use this instead of sed/awk in terminal. Uses fuzzy matching (9 strategies) so minor whitespace/indentation differences won't break it. Returns a unified diff. Auto-runs syntax checks after editing.

REPLACE MODE (mode='replace', default): find a unique string and replace it. REQUIRED PARAMETERS: mode, path, old_string, new_string.
PATCH MODE (mode='patch'): apply V4A multi-file patches for bulk changes. REQUIRED PARAMETERS: mode, patch.

mode.description: Edit mode. 'replace' (default): requires path + old_string + new_string. 'patch': requires patch content only.

path.description: REQUIRED when mode='replace'. File path to edit.

old_string.description: REQUIRED when mode='replace'. Exact text to find and replace. Must be unique in the file unless replace_all=true. Include surrounding context lines to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: REQUIRED when mode='replace'. Replacement text. Pass empty string '' to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: Replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false)

patch.description:
REQUIRED when mode='patch'. V4A format patch content. Format:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: path/to/file
@@ context hint @@
 context line
-removed line
+added line
*** End Patch

function.name: process
function.description: Manage background processes started with terminal(background=true). Actions: 'list' (show all), 'poll' (check status + new output), 'log' (full output with pagination), 'wait' (block until done or timeout), 'kill' (terminate), 'write' (send raw stdin data without newline), 'submit' (send data + Enter, for answering prompts), 'close' (close stdin/send EOF).

action.description: Action to perform on background processes

session_id.description: Process session ID (from terminal background output). Required for all actions except 'list'.

data.description: Text to send to process stdin (for 'write' and 'submit' actions)

timeout.description: Max seconds to block for 'wait' action. Returns partial output on timeout.

offset.description: Line offset for 'log' action (default: last 200 lines)

limit.description: Max lines to return for 'log' action

function.name: read_file
function.description: Read a text file with line numbers and pagination. Use this instead of cat/head/tail in terminal. Output format: 'LINE_NUM|CONTENT'. Suggests similar filenames if not found. Use offset and limit for large files. Reads exceeding ~100K characters are rejected; use offset and limit to read specific sections of large files. NOTE: Cannot read images or binary files — use vision_analyze for images.

path.description: Path to the file to read (absolute, relative, or ~/path)

function.name: search_files
function.description:
Search file contents or find files by name. Use this instead of grep/rg/find/ls in terminal. Ripgrep-backed, faster than shell equivalents.

Content search (target='content'): Regex search inside files. Output modes: full matches with line numbers, file paths only, or match counts.

File search (target='files'): Find files by glob pattern (e.g., '*.py', '*config*'). Also use this instead of ls — results sorted by modification time.

pattern.description: Regex pattern for content search, or glob pattern (e.g., '*.py') for file search

target.description: 'content' searches inside file contents, 'files' searches for files by name

path.description: Directory or file to search in (default: current working directory)

file_glob.description: Filter files by pattern in grep mode (e.g., '*.py' to only search Python files)

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 50)

offset.description: Skip first N results for pagination (default: 0)

output_mode.description: Output format for grep mode: 'content' shows matching lines with line numbers, 'files_only' lists file paths, 'count' shows match counts per file

context.description: Number of context lines before and after each match (grep mode only)

function.name: terminal
function.description:
Execute shell commands on a Linux environment. Filesystem usually persists between calls.

Do NOT use cat/head/tail to read files — use read_file instead.
Do NOT use grep/rg/find to search — use search_files instead.
Do NOT use ls to list directories — use search_files(target='files') instead.
Do NOT use sed/awk to edit files — use patch instead.
Do NOT use echo/cat heredoc to create files — use write_file instead.
Reserve terminal for: builds, installs, git, processes, scripts, network, package managers, and anything that needs a shell.

Foreground (default): Commands return INSTANTLY when done, even if the timeout is high. Set timeout=300 for long builds/scripts — you'll still get the result in seconds if it's fast. Prefer foreground for short commands.
Background: Set background=true to get a session_id. Two patterns:
  (1) Long-lived processes that never exit (servers, watchers).
  (2) Long-running tasks with notify_on_complete=true — you can keep working on other things and the system auto-notifies you when the task finishes. Great for test suites, builds, deployments, or anything that takes more than a minute.
For servers/watchers, do NOT use shell-level background wrappers (nohup/disown/setsid/trailing '&') in foreground mode. Use background=true so Hermes can track lifecycle and output.
After starting a server, verify readiness with a health check or log signal, then run tests in a separate terminal() call. Avoid blind sleep loops.
Use process(action="poll") for progress checks, process(action="wait") to block until done.
Working directory: Use 'workdir' for per-command cwd.
PTY mode: Set pty=true for interactive CLI tools (Codex, Claude Code, Python REPL).

Do NOT use vim/nano/interactive tools without pty=true — they hang without a pseudo-terminal. Pipe git output to cat if it might page.

command.description: The command to execute on the VM

background.description: Run the command in the background. Two patterns: (1) Long-lived processes that never exit (servers, watchers). (2) Long-running tasks paired with notify_on_complete=true — you can keep working and get notified when the task finishes. For short commands, prefer foreground with a generous timeout instead.

timeout.description: Max seconds to wait (default: 180, foreground max: 600). Returns INSTANTLY when command finishes — set high for long tasks, you won't wait unnecessarily. Foreground timeout above 600s is rejected; use background=true for longer commands.

workdir.description: Working directory for this command (absolute path). Defaults to the session working directory.

pty.description: Run in pseudo-terminal (PTY) mode for interactive CLI tools like Codex, Claude Code, or Python REPL. Only works with local and SSH backends. Default: false.

notify_on_complete.description: When true (and background=true), you'll be automatically notified exactly once when the process finishes. **This is the right choice for almost every long-running task** — tests, builds, deployments, multi-item batch jobs, anything that takes over a minute and has a defined end. Use this and keep working on other things; the system notifies you on exit. MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE with watch_patterns — when both are set, watch_patterns is dropped.

watch_patterns.description: Strings to watch for in background process output. HARD RATE LIMIT: at most 1 notification per 15 seconds per process — matches arriving inside the cooldown are dropped. After 3 consecutive 15-second windows with dropped matches, watch_patterns is automatically disabled for that process and promoted to notify_on_complete behavior (one notification on exit, no more mid-process spam). USE ONLY for truly rare, one-shot mid-process signals on LONG-LIVED processes that will never exit on their own — e.g. ['Application startup complete'] on a server so you know when to hit its endpoint, or ['migration done'] on a daemon. DO NOT use for: (1) end-of-run markers like 'DONE'/'PASS' — use notify_on_complete instead; (2) error patterns like 'ERROR'/'Traceback' in loops or multi-item batch jobs — they fire on every iteration and you'll hit the strike limit fast; (3) anything you'd ever combine with notify_on_complete. When in doubt, choose notify_on_complete. MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE with notify_on_complete — set one, not both.

function.name: write_file
function.description: Write content to a file, completely replacing existing content. Use this instead of echo/cat heredoc in terminal. Creates parent directories automatically. OVERWRITES the entire file — use 'patch' for targeted edits. Auto-runs syntax checks on .py/.json/.yaml/.toml and other linted languages; only NEW errors introduced by this write are surfaced (pre-existing errors are filtered out).

path.description: Path to the file to write (will be created if it doesn't exist, overwritten if it does)

content.description: Complete content to write to the file

# El Viajero — AI Agent Guide

## Quick Links
- **Live:** https://el-viajero.paragu-ai.com
- **Repo:** github.com/Ai-Whisperers/elviajero-comercio
- **Local:** /root/elviajero
- **Docker service:** elviajero_web (2 replicas)
- **VPS:** 72.61.44.159
- **Traefik:** Host(`el-viajero.paragu-ai.com`) || Host(`viajero.paragu-ai.com`)

## Architecture
Cloudflare (DNS, SSL) → VPS → Traefik → elviajero_web:3000

## Pages
12 pages: Home, Tienda (catalog), Blog (6 posts), Nosotros, Contacto, FAQ, Promociones, Privacidad, Términos, Sitemap

## Design System
Green (#1B5E20) + orange (#E65100) on light background. Fonts: Inter. Voice: practical, adventurous, Spanish/English/Guaraní.

## Data Layer
**Supabase** (PostgreSQL) — shared project `qyvokpribmbrosafntqa`
- Customer auth: Supabase Auth (email/password, Google, Facebook)
- Tables: profiles, products, categories, orders, addresses, promo_codes, reviews, subscribers, abandoned_carts, stock_alerts
- Admin auth: profiles.role = 'admin'
- Supabase clients: `lib/supabase/server.ts` (SSR), `lib/supabase/browser.ts` (client), `lib/supabase/admin.ts` (service_role)
- Migration: `supabase/migrations/001_elviajero_schema.sql`
- RLS policies on all tables

## Content (Static)
Content in `content/es.json` (and other locales) for page copy.
Dynamic data (products, orders) comes from Supabase.

## Build & Deploy
```bash
npm run build
docker build -t elviajero:prod .
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml elviajero --with-registry-auth
```

## Critical Patterns
- All products in content/es.json (12 products, 6 categories)
- WhatsApp-first ordering (all CTAs go to WhatsApp with prefilled templates)
- Currency toggle (PYG/USD) with localStorage persistence
- Language toggle (ES/EN/GN)
- Dark mode toggle
- Full admin panel

## Client Onboarding
See `docs/client-questionnaire.md` for the full onboarding questionnaire.
See `docs/brand-guide.md` for brand identity details.

## Core Architecture

- **Framework:** Next.js 16 App Router (React 19, TypeScript 5)
- **Content:** Purely file-based — `content/{locale}.json`, `nexa-pages/{slug}.json`, `images.json`, `site.json`, `testimonials.json`
- **No database:** All content loads from JSON files via `src/lib/page-data.ts` (no Supabase)
- **Loading:** In-memory LRU cache with 30s TTL
- **Section overrides:** Component overrides live in `src/components/` and are registered in `src/components/SectionsRenderer.tsx`
- **Locales:** 4 complete — ES, EN, NL, DE (all 34 sections, all properly translated)
- **Styling:** Tailwind CSS v4 with `@theme` tokens in `src/app/globals.css`

## Key Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `content/{locale}.json` | All text content per locale (34 sections each) |
| `nexa-pages/{slug}.json` | Page configs — section ordering + content key references |
| `images.json` | Image manifest — 82+ entries across 14 categories |
| `site.json` | Domain, features, booking URL, social links |
| `src/lib/page-data.ts` | File-based content loader (no Supabase) |
| `src/components/SectionsRenderer.tsx` | Section registry with overrides |
| `src/components/ProcessSection.tsx` | Timeline component override |
| `src/components/TeamSection.tsx` | Team cards override |
| `src/components/StorySection.tsx` | Story layout override |
| `src/components/PageHeroSection.tsx` | Hero section override |
| `src/components/CtaBanner.tsx` | CTA banner override |
| `src/components/BlogSection.tsx` | Blog grid override |
| `src/components/FaqSection.tsx` | FAQ with categories override |
| `src/components/BookingEmbedSection.tsx` | Booking section override |
| `src/components/ContactDetailsSection.tsx` | Contact card override |
| `src/components/ExitPopup.tsx` | Exit popup (strings from content JSON) |

## Build & Deploy

### Local Development
```bash
npm run dev          # Dev server with Turbopack (compiles in ~350ms)
npm run build        # Production build
npm run start        # Production server
```

### Docker Deploy (VPS)
```bash
npm run build
docker build -t nexa-paraguay:prod --no-cache .
docker service update --force --image nexa-paraguay:prod nexa_web
```

### Screenshots
```bash
npm run screenshots  # Captures 22 pages
```

{"content": "     1|import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from "next/server"
     2|import { createClient } from "@supabase/supabase-js"
     3|
     4|function getAdminClient() {
     5|  const url = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL || ""
     6|  // Use the service role key directly as both apikey and auth bearer
     7|  // The sb_secret format works when passed as the second arg to createClient
     8|  const serviceKey = process.env.SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY || ""
     9|  if (!url) throw new Error("NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL not set")
    10|  // Works with both old JWT and new sb_secret format
    11|  return createClient(url, serviceKey, {
    12|    auth: { persistSession: false },
    13|  })
    14|}
    15|
    16|export async function GET(req: NextRequest) {
    17|  const supabase = getAdminClient()
    18|  const { searchParams } = new URL(req.url)
    19|  const page = parseInt(searchParams.get("page") || "1")
    20|  const perPage = parseInt(searchParams.get("perPage") || "20")
    21|  const from = (page - 1) * perPage
    22|  const to = from + perPage - 1
    23|
    24|  const { data, error, count } = await supabase
    25|    .from("ej_products")
    26|    .select("*", { count: "exact" })
    27|    .order("name")
    28|    .range(from, to)
    29|  if (error) return NextResponse.json({ error: error.message }, { status: 500 })
    30|  return NextResponse.json({ data: data ?? [], total: count ?? 0, page, perPage })
    31|}
    32|
    33|export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {
    34|  const supabase = getAdminClient()
    35|  const body = await req.json()
    36|  const { data, error } = await supabase.from("ej_products").insert(body).select()
    37|  if (error) return NextResponse.json({ error: error.message }, { status: 500 })
    38|  return NextResponse.json(data?.[0] ?? null)
    39|}
    40|
    41|export async function PATCH(req: NextRequest) {
    42|  const supabase = getAdminClient()
    43|  const body = await req.json()
    44|  const { id, ...updates } = body
    45|  if (!id) return NextResponse.json({ error: "id required" }, { status: 400 })
    46|  const { data, error } = await supabase.from("ej_products").update(updates).eq("id", id).select()
    47|  if (error) return NextResponse.json({ error: error.message }, { status: 500 })
    48|  return NextResponse.json(data?.[0] ?? null)
    49|}

    52|  const supabase = getAdminClient()
    53|  const { searchParams } = new URL(req.url)
    54|  const id = searchParams.get("id")
    55|  if (!id) return NextResponse.json({ error: "id required" }, { status: 400 })
    56|  const { error } = await supabase.from("ej_products").delete().eq("id", id)
    57|  if (error) return NextResponse.json({ error: error.message }, { status: 500 })
    58|  return NextResponse.json({ success: true })

{"content": "     1|import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from "next/server"
     2|import { createAdminClient } from "@ai-whisperers/auth/supabase/admin"
     3|
     4|// Blog posts stored in ej_site_config under key "blog_posts"
     5|const KEY = "blog_posts"
     6|
     7|export async function GET() {
     8|  const supabase = createAdminClient()
     9|  const { data, error } = await supabase
    10|    .from("ej_site_config")
    11|    .select("value")
    12|    .eq("key", KEY)
    13|    .single()
    14|  
    15|  if (error && error.code !== "PGRST116") {
    16|    return NextResponse.json({ error: error.message }, { status: 500 })
    17|  }
    18|  
    19|  const posts: any[] = data?.value ?? []
    20|  return NextResponse.json(posts.sort((a: any, b: any) => (b.created_at || "").localeCompare(a.created_at || "")))
    21|}
    22|
    23|export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {
    24|  const supabase = createAdminClient()
    25|  const body = await req.json()
    26|  
    27|  // Get existing posts, add new one
    28|  const { data: existing } = await supabase
    29|    .from("ej_site_config")
    30|    .select("value")
    31|    .eq("key", KEY)
    32|    .single()
    33|  
    34|  const posts: any[] = existing?.value ?? []
    35|  
    36|  // Check slug uniqueness
    37|  if (posts.find(p => p.slug === body.slug)) {
    38|    return NextResponse.json({ error: "Ya existe un post con ese slug" }, { status: 400 })
    39|  }
    40|  
    41|  posts.push({
    42|    slug: body.slug,
    43|    title: body.title,
    44|    excerpt: body.excerpt || "",
    45|    content: body.content || "",
    46|    category: body.category || "general",
    47|    image_url: body.image_url || "",
    48|    author: body.author || "",
    49|    published: body.published || false,
    50|    created_at: body.created_at || new Date().toISOString().split("T")[0],
    51|  })
    52|  
    53|  const { error } = await supabase
    54|    .from("ej_site_config")
    55|    .upsert({ key: KEY, value: posts }, { onConflict: "key" })
    56|  
    57|  if (error) return NextResponse.json({ error: error.message }, { status: 500 })
    58|  return NextResponse.json({ success: true })
    59|}
    60|
    61|export async function PATCH(req: NextRequest) {
    62|  const supabase = createAdminClient()
    63|  const body = await req.json()
    64|  const slug = body.original_slug || body.slug
    65|  
    66|  const { data: existing } = await supabase
    67|    .from("ej_site_config")
    68|    .select("value")
    69|    .eq("key", KEY)
    70|    .single()
    71|  
    72|  const posts: any[] = existing?.value ?? []
    73|  const idx = posts.findIndex(p => p.slug === slug)
    74|  if (idx === -1) return NextResponse.json({ error: "Post not found" }, { status: 404 })
    75|  
    76|  posts[idx] = { ...posts[idx], ...body }
    77|  
    78|  const { error } = await supabase
    79|    .from("ej_site_config")
    80|    .upsert({ key: KEY, value: posts }, { onConflict: "key" })
    81|  
    82|  if (error) return NextResponse.json({ error: error.message }, { status: 500 })
    83|  return NextResponse.json({ success: true })
    84|}
    85|
    86|export async function DELETE(req: NextRequest) {
    87|  const supabase = createAdminClient()
    88|  const slug = new URL(req.url).searchParams.get("slug")
    89|  if (!slug) return NextResponse.json({ error: "slug required" }, { status: 400 })
    90|  
    91|  const { data: existing } = await supabase
    92|    .from("ej_site_config")
    93|    .select("value")
    94|    .eq("key", KEY)
    95|    .single()
    96|  
    97|  const posts: any[] = existing?.value ?? []
    98|  const filtered = posts.filter(p => p.slug !== slug)
    99|  
   100|  const { error } = await supabase
   101|    .from("ej_site_config")
   102|    .upsert({ key: KEY, value: filtered }, { onConflict: "key" })
   103|  
   104|  if (error) return NextResponse.json({ error: error.message }, { status: 500 })
   105|  return NextResponse.json({ success: true })
   106|}

     3|export function createAdminClient() {
     4|  const url = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL || ""
     5|  // The apikey header must be a JWT. Use the old anon key for apikey,
     6|  // then override to service role via Authorization header.
     7|  const anonJwt = "eyJhbG...SXRg"
     8|  const serviceKey = process.env.SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY || ""
     9|  if (!url) throw new Error("NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL not set")
    10|
    11|  return createClient(url, anonJwt, {
    12|    auth: { persistSession: false },
    13|    global: serviceKey
    14|      ? { headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${serviceKey}` } }
    15|      : {},
    16|  })

     2|-- Migration 001: Create all tables with RLS policies
     3|
     4|-- 1. profiles (extends auth.users)
     5|CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.profiles (
     6|  id UUID PRIMARY KEY REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
     7|  name TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
     8|  phone TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
     9|  role TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'customer' CHECK (role IN ('customer', 'admin')),
    10|  avatar_url TEXT DEFAULT '',
    11|  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    12|);
    13|
    14|ALTER TABLE public.profiles ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
    15|
    16|-- 2. products
    17|CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.products (
    18|  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    19|  name TEXT NOT NULL,
    20|  category TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
    21|  price TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '0',
    22|  price_before TEXT DEFAULT '',
    23|  description TEXT DEFAULT '',
    24|  brand TEXT DEFAULT '',
    25|  specs TEXT DEFAULT '',
    26|  stock INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    27|  weight TEXT DEFAULT '',
    28|  image_url TEXT DEFAULT '',
    29|  is_new BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,
    30|  featured BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,
    31|  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    32|);
    33|
    34|ALTER TABLE public.products ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
    35|
    36|-- 3. categories
    37|CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.categories (
    38|  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    39|  name TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    40|  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    41|);
    42|
    43|ALTER TABLE public.categories ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
    44|
    45|-- 4. orders
    46|CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.orders (
    47|  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    48|  user_id UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    49|  items JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]'::jsonb,

    61|CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.addresses (

    74|
    75|ALTER TABLE public.addresses ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
    76|
    77|-- 6. promo_codes
    78|CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.promo_codes (

    87|);
    88|
    89|ALTER TABLE public.promo_codes ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
    90|
    91|-- 7. reviews
    92|CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.reviews (
    93|  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),

    97|  text TEXT DEFAULT '',

    99|);
   100|

   102|

   107|);
   108|

   110|

   159|RETURNS void
   160|LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER

## COMPREHENSIVE ADMIN PANEL AUDIT

### 1. EL VIAJERO — Full Admin Panel Analysis

#### 1.1 Supabase Client Pattern

There are **two different Supabase admin clients** used:
- **Products route** (`app/api/admin/products/route.ts`): Inline `createClient` with `serviceKey` from env — creates its own client directly
- **All other routes** (`orders`, `customers`, `categories`, `promos`, `reviews`, `blog`, `subscribers`, `users`, `content`, `theme`, `b2b`, `bulk`): Use `createAdminClient()` from `@ai-whisperers/auth/supabase/admin` (packages/auth/supabase/admin.ts)

The shared `createAdminClient()` uses a **hardcoded anon JWT** for the apikey header and overrides Authorization with the service role key. This is a security concern — the hardcoded JWT (`eyJhbG...SXRg`) is truncated/expired.

**Issue**: Products route uses a different pattern — it re-initializes Supabase inline instead of using the shared admin client. Both ultimately hit the same project but the products route lacks error handling on env vars.

#### 1.2 Admin Pages Summary Table

| # | Page | Path | CRUD | API Route | Supabase Table | Data Source |
|---|------|------|------|-----------|----------------|-------------|
| 1 | **Dashboard** | `/admin` | R (stats + recent orders) | `/api/admin/stats` | `profiles`, `ej_products`, `ej_orders` | ✅ Supabase |
| 2 | **Orders List** | `/admin/pedidos` | R, U (status, notes) | `/api/admin/orders` (GET, PATCH) | `ej_orders` | ✅ Supabase |
| 3 | **Order Detail** | `/admin/pedidos/detalle?id=X` | R | `/api/admin/orders?id=X` (GET) | `ej_orders` | ✅ Supabase |
| 4 | **Products** | `/admin/productos` | **C, R, U, D** + Duplicate + Bulk | `/api/admin/products` (GET, POST, PATCH, DELETE) + `/api/admin/products/bulk` (POST) | `ej_products` | ✅ Supabase |
| 5 | **Categories** | `/admin/categorias` | **C, R, D** | `/api/admin/categories` (GET, POST, DELETE) | `ej_categories` | ✅ Supabase |
| 6 | **Customers** | `/admin/clientes` | R (read profiles + orders) | `/api/admin/customers` (GET), `/api/admin/orders` (GET) | `profiles`, `ej_orders` | ✅ Supabase |
| 7 | **Users** | `/admin/usuarios` | R | `/api/admin/users` (GET) | `profiles` | ✅ Supabase |
| 8 | **Promos** | `/admin/promos` | **C, R, U, D** | `/api/admin/promos` (GET, POST, PATCH, DELETE) | `ej_promo_codes` | ✅ Supabase |
| 9 | **Reviews** | `/admin/resenas` | R, D | `/api/admin/reviews` (GET, DELETE) | `ej_reviews` | ✅ Supabase |
| 10 | **Subscribers** | `/admin/suscriptores` | R | `/api/admin/subscribers` (GET) | `ej_subscribers` | ✅ Supabase |
| 11 | **Blog Posts** | `/admin/blog` | **C, R, U, D** | `/api/admin/blog` (GET, POST, PATCH, DELETE) | `ej_site_config` (JSONB in single row, key="blog_posts") | ✅ Supabase (config table) |
| 12 | **Content Editor** | `/admin/contenido` | R, U (read/update overrides) | `/api/admin/content` (GET, POST) | `ej_site_config` (JSONB, key="content_overrides") | ✅ Supabase (config table) |
| 13 | **Theme** | `/admin/tema` | R, U (read/update theme preset) | `/api/admin/theme` (GET, POST) | `ej_admin_themes` | ✅ Supabase |
| 14 | **B2B Customers** | `/admin/b2b` | **C, R, U** (toggle status) | `/api/admin/b2b` (GET, POST, PATCH) | `ej_b2b_customers` | ✅ Supabase |
| 15 | **Photos** | `/admin/fotos` | U (upload images to products) | Reuses `/api/admin/products` (PATCH) + Supabase Storage (`ej_images` bucket) | `ej_products` + Storage | ✅ Supabase + Storage |
| 16 | **Enrich (AI)** | `/admin/enriquecer` | U (update product descriptions via AI) | `/api/admin/enrich` (POST — AI generation) + `/api/admin/products` (PATCH) | `ej_products` | ✅ Supabase (AI enrichment pass-through) |
| 17 | **Import (CSV)** | `/admin/importar` | C (batch product insert) | `/api/admin/products/bulk` (POST) | `ej_products` | ✅ Supabase |
| 18 | **Reports** | `/admin/reportes` | R (read orders, compute analytics) | `/api/admin/orders` (GET) | `ej_orders` | ✅ Supabase |

#### 1.3 Database API Routes (Legacy)

| Route | Current State | Supabase Table |
|-------|---------------|----------------|
| `/api/db/products` (GET, POST) | **Legacy** — reads/writes `ej_products`. Uses action pattern (`action: 'update'`). The admin panel does NOT use these; only potential legacy callers. | `ej_products` |
| `/api/db/promos` (GET, POST) | **Legacy** — reads/writes `ej_promo_codes`. Uses action pattern (`action: 'create'/'delete'`). Not used by admin panel. | `ej_promo_codes` |

#### 1.4 Schema Mismatches & Issues

| Page | Issue | Severity |
|------|-------|----------|
